plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate extreme risk.
Increase allocation to QQQI for income and stability.
Trim SPY and CRWD to reduce concentration and manage risk.
Add XLF, a defensive sector with a strong setup, for diversification.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a significant cash buffer.
Expect lower volatility and a more stable portfolio.
conservative.read: This mode aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5% monthly return. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, holding a larger cash buffer for stability. Drawdowns are expected to be minimal.

plan: Trim SOXL and sell SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF risk.
Trim SPY to meet the single name concentration limit.
Increase QQQ allocation to balance broad market exposure.
Slightly trim CRWD to further diversify within tech.
Add PANW, a top-rated software name, for growth potential.
This plan balances growth with risk reduction.
balanced.read: This mode targets ~2.5% monthly return through risk-first growth. It trims leverage and concentration while adding quality names and broad index exposure. Expect moderate growth with controlled drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to reduce concentration and increase flexibility.
Sell QQQI to shift focus from income to aggressive growth.
Trim SMH to reduce exposure to semiconductors showing weakness.
Increase SOXL and add TECS to leverage semiconductor trends.
Add NVDA, a high-scoring tech name, for concentrated growth.
This plan maximizes growth potential with higher risk.
aggressive.read: This aggressive mode targets ~4% monthly return by leaning into leverage and concentration. It increases leveraged ETFs and high-scoring tech names, accepting higher potential upside and significant drawdown risk. Daily rebalancing of leveraged ETFs can impact performance.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to remove high risk.
Sell high-beta single names (CRWD, SMH) for stability.
Maximize QQQI allocation for consistent monthly income distributions.
Increase SPY allocation for broad market exposure and stability.
This plan prioritizes steady income and low drawdowns.
Expect reliable monthly distributions with reduced growth potential.
income.read: This income-focused mode targets ~1% monthly return through steady distributions. It eliminates leverage and high-beta names, concentrating on income ETFs and broad indices. NAV can still decline in market sell-offs, but the focus is on reliable income over growth.
